data.description:
## Context

Four test runners coexist in DSO: bash-runner.sh, suite-engine.sh, run-hook-tests.sh, and validate.sh. They have drifted on RED-marker tolerance, command_hash tracking, scope coverage, and timeout budget. Each drift was caught reactively post-merge — the bash-runner is updated after a CI failure, not as part of the same change that updated suite-engine. There is no convergence test asserting that all runners produce equivalent classification (pass/fail/skip) for the same input.

This epic delivers the standing equivalence contract test that closes the drift class. Pairs with the ab57-b534 scope expansion (G2-M2), which mandates planner-level corpus updates whenever runner code is touched — this epic is the receiving home for those corpus contributions.

See docs/observed-failure-patterns-4-26.md Pattern 2 and docs/process-gaps-4-26.md G2.

## Cited evidence

Pattern 2 — Test-runner / gate semantic divergence (~15% of recent fixes):
- 7225-7708 (commit 48a84914e7): bash-runner.sh does not honor SUITE_TEST_INDEX RED markers that suite-engine tolerates; ~25 false-positive failures vs CI; blocks scripts/release.sh precondition #8
- bf39-4494 (commit dc53a44c51): validate.sh --ci infinite PENDING loop because per-file timeout >45s under sequential runner
- e2b6-1059 (commit d1f7877896): validate.sh test scope diverges from CI workflow (validate covers tests/skills, CI covers only tests/hooks + tests/scripts) — main stays green while skills tests fail
- e8a9-136f (commit 9b073eb686): figma-pullback test missing RED-marker registration
- 8305-2091 (open/recent): test-status recording with source-file mode preserves stale failures — fixed tests not re-run
- 610f-c021 (open): test-status recorder blocked by pre-bash-functions.sh guard within /dso:commit workflow

Structural evidence (G2):
- plugins/dso/scripts/runners/bash-runner.sh:131-133 — comments acknowledge mirroring suite-engine semantics, but the mirror is maintained by hand
- plugins/dso/docs/workflows/REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md:54-116 — reviewer Step 1 runs validate.sh --ci only; does not also run the suite-engine path or compare outputs
- plugins/dso/skills/sprint/SKILL.md — sprint orchestrator dispatches test execution but has no step asserting "if you touched bash-runner.sh, also run the suite-engine equivalence check"

Discovery channel pattern: all four cited Pattern 2 tickets were caught either by validate.sh --ci (release-time integration test) or by CI itself, never by pre-commit. Each fix was authored as a standalone change without a convergence test.

## Mitigations included

- G2-M1 — Create tests/runners/test-runner-equivalence.sh that takes a fixed corpus of test files (known-passing set, known-RED-marker set, known-skipping set) and runs each through every runner declared in plugins/dso/scripts/runners/. Asserts all runners produce the same pass/fail/skip classification for each file. Add the test to validate.sh --ci. Add new pre-commit hook runner-touch-check.sh that fires only when the diff touches a file under plugins/dso/scripts/runners/ and runs the equivalence test before the commit.

## Proposed success criteria

1. A fixed corpus exists under tests/runners/fixtures/ covering the three classification axes (passing, RED-marker, skipping); corpus is documented and stable
2. Equivalence test asserts identical classification across all declared runners; runs in validate.sh --ci
3. Pre-commit hook runner-touch-check.sh runs the equivalence test only when the diff touches runner code; failure blocks the commit
4. New runner additions are detected automatically (the test enumerates runners from a manifest at plugins/dso/config/test-runners.yaml, not a hardcoded list)
5. Each cited drift ticket (7225-7708, bf39-4494, e2b6-1059, e8a9-136f) corresponds to a regression case in the corpus; re-running the equivalence test against pre-fix runner code reproduces the drift
6. Test runtime budget: equivalence test completes in under 30s on macOS local; under 60s in CI

## Coordination notes

- Pairs with ab57-b534's new sub-criterion (G2-M2 absorbed there): planner mandates a corpus update in any runner-touching task, and this epic delivers the corpus + test that the planner references
- Pairs with New Epic B (G2-M3 reviewer rubric for runner changes): reviewer rubric and equivalence test both fire on the same diff trigger — independent rings of defense
- Coexists with cf7b-86a9's shellcheck/ruff parity work — different surface (test classification semantics vs static lint coverage)

## References

- docs/observed-failure-patterns-4-26.md Pattern 2
- docs/process-gaps-4-26.md G2
- docs/proposed-mitigations-4-26.md G2-M1
- docs/new-epics-4-26.md § New Epic D
data.title: Cross-runner equivalence contract testing
